title: Diasperus sellowianus
intro: Diasperus sellowianus, also known as Jaborandi, is a plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the family Myoporaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate hair growth, reduce dandruff, and promote scalp health, making it a popular treatment for alopecia and other scalp conditions. The bioactive constituents of Diasperus sellowianus include the alkaloids pilocarpine and jaborine, as well as other compounds that contribute to its medicinal effects. Jaborandi is often prepared as a tincture, ointment, or shampoo, and is used in traditional medicine to promote hair growth and improve scalp health.
taxonomy: Diasperus sellowianus is a species within the family Apocynaceae, which includes a variety of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. The genus Diasperus belongs to the subfamily Plumerioideae, a group of plants characterized by their showy flowers and tubular shapes. The classification of Diasperus sellowianus is further divided into the order Gentianales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. Diasperus sellowianus is also known to be a part of the class Magnoliopsida, which comprises the majority of flowering plant species. Further classification of Diasperus sellowianus involves the division of the kingdom Plantae, the most basic level of classification for plant species.
